title: What To Expect When Barts Expecting

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer experiencing some mishap or frustration
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase of exasperation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but low originality since it's been used thousands of times

jokes.text: D'oh! Ooh!
jokes.setup: Homer's first D'oh suggests initial frustration
jokes.punchline: The 'Ooh!' suggests additional pain or realization
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Homer's pain/frustration, but very basic and repetitive

jokes.text: Why, you little...!
jokes.setup: Homer addressing Bart with his signature threat
jokes.punchline: The incomplete threat that we all know ends with strangling
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer-Bart dynamic setup - the incomplete threat is funnier than completion

jokes.text: Eh, welcome fellow barkeeps, gin-slingers, and beer jerks.
jokes.setup: Formal meeting of bartenders with increasingly casual terms
jokes.punchline: 'Beer jerks' as the least dignified description
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of terms from formal to crude, fits Moe's character

jokes.text: Mom-and-pop bartenders can't compete with big box stores like Booze Barn and Hooch City.
jokes.setup: Economic struggles of small businesses
jokes.punchline: Absurd big-box store names for alcohol
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of big box stores applied to alcohol retail

jokes.text: Uh, folks, this is the owner of the airport bar speaking. Uh, we're experiencing some financial turbulence.
jokes.setup: Airport bar owner addressing customers
jokes.punchline: Using aviation terminology for financial problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific wordplay - airport bar owner naturally uses aviation metaphors

jokes.text: Nick Fury, Agent of Schnapps; Sex in the Batmobile; and Wolveriskey.
jokes.setup: Creating superhero-themed drink names
jokes.punchline: Three clever portmanteau drinks mixing superheroes with alcohol
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay - three distinct approaches to superhero/alcohol puns, very quotable

jokes.text: Here, check out my, uh, portfolium here.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to sound professional
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'portfolio' as 'portfolium'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe malapropism - shows his attempt to sound educated while revealing he's not

jokes.text: Flame on! Oh-God-oh-God-oh-God!
jokes.setup: Someone yelling Fantastic Four's Human Torch catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Immediately panicking, suggesting they actually caught fire
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - superhero catchphrase followed by real-world consequences

jokes.text: Why do they call this a yard of ale? Easy. After you drink one, you're passed out in your yard.
jokes.setup: Question about the name 'yard of ale'
jokes.punchline: False etymology based on where you end up after drinking it
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - sounds like it will explain the real etymology but gives drunk logic instead

jokes.text: Well, better get home. The delightfulness ended hours ago.
jokes.setup: Homer at the end of a bar crawl
jokes.punchline: Admitting the fun stopped long ago but he kept drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - acknowledging he's no longer having fun but continuing anyway

jokes.text: Oh, why are you doing this to me, booze? I drank every kind of you!
jokes.setup: Homer suffering from drinking
jokes.punchline: Addressing alcohol directly and claiming loyalty through variety
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer personification of inanimate objects plus his twisted logic of loyalty

jokes.text: Wait, I think I just sucked up a dollar!
jokes.setup: Bart apparently using some kind of suction device
jokes.punchline: Accidentally ingesting money
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy - mildly amusing but not particularly clever

jokes.text: José, hit the switch! We are not all Josés, man.
jokes.setup: Someone yelling a command to 'José'
jokes.punchline: Worker correcting the assumption that all Hispanic workers are named José
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with perfect economy - calls out stereotyping

jokes.text: Every time I do art, some do-gooder teacher sends it straight to a therapist.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about art class
jokes.punchline: His art is so disturbing it triggers psychological concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart line - reveals his troublemaker nature while showing adult concern

jokes.text: Your stuff is pretty disturbing. I've never acted on any of it.
jokes.setup: Teacher acknowledging Bart's disturbing art
jokes.punchline: 'I've never acted on any of it' suggests the teacher has violent thoughts too
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark twist - teacher accidentally reveals her own violent thoughts

jokes.text: And you shall leave immortal! Also, take out the trash.
jokes.setup: Grand proclamation about art making someone immortal
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by mundane trash duty
jokes.explanation: Classic high-low juxtaposition - artistic inspiration immediately undercut by chores

jokes.text: Really? Wonderful? I just drowned a grasshopper in paint and let him crawl on the paper till he died.
jokes.setup: Art teacher praising Bart's work as wonderful
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutal description of his 'artistic' process
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disturbing Bart moment - casual cruelty presented as bewildering art

jokes.text: You know what that's called? Mixed-media conceptual art.
jokes.setup: Bart's description of killing an insect
jokes.punchline: Teacher reframing animal cruelty as sophisticated art form
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of pretentious art interpretation - turns cruelty into high art

jokes.text: Oh, then it's protest art! Stop encouraging me!
jokes.setup: Bart says he hates art, teacher calls it mixed-media
jokes.punchline: Teacher doubles down calling it protest art, Bart begs her to stop
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - teacher's relentless positivity drives Bart to desperation

jokes.text: Training for the Olympics.
jokes.setup: Question about what she's doing (presumably something mundane)
jokes.punchline: Absurdly grandiose explanation for ordinary activity
jokes.explanation: Basic deadpan humor - mildly amusing but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I wanna make somebody mad.
jokes.setup: Shauna asking Bart to kiss her
jokes.punchline: Her motivation is just to anger someone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shauna character moment - using romance as manipulation tool

jokes.text: Just like the chicken foot say you would!
jokes.setup: Customer choosing plastic over paper bag
jokes.punchline: Voodoo queen claiming her chicken foot predicted this mundane choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - mystical powers applied to trivial retail choices

jokes.text: Art teachers don't have a desk. They just throw their purse anywhere.
jokes.setup: Question about how she got DNA from teacher's purse
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about art teachers' disorganization
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about teacher stereotypes

jokes.text: Just had to be tear of chum.
jokes.setup: Voodoo spell requiring disgusting ingredient
jokes.punchline: Bart's resignation to having to use gross fish bait tears
jokes.explanation: Mild gross-out humor with Bart's characteristic reluctant acceptance

jokes.text: Orchestra?! On an art teacher's salary? That's black magic!
jokes.setup: Finding expensive theater tickets in art teacher's purse
jokes.punchline: Bart declaring good seats impossible on teacher salary must be supernatural
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about teacher salaries with perfect ironic setup

jokes.text: I won't bore you with the dark details, but let's just say she's in no condition for teaching.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming to have cast voodoo spell on teacher
jokes.punchline: Ominous suggestion that something terrible happened
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation of something sinister before pregnancy reveal

jokes.text: A very special tummy ache.
jokes.setup: Teacher saying she has a tummy ache
jokes.punchline: The ominous qualifier suggesting it's not ordinary
jokes.explanation: Sets up pregnancy reveal with euphemistic language

jokes.text: Aah! Bart Simpson got a teacher pregnant!
jokes.setup: Teacher announces pregnancy after Bart's voodoo spell
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent but shocking misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph line - innocent child logic creating scandalous interpretation

jokes.text: Typical Ralph nonsense. Bart Simpson got a teacher pregnant!
jokes.setup: One student dismissing Ralph's outburst as nonsense
jokes.punchline: Immediately repeating the exact same 'nonsense'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - dismissing Ralph while spreading the same rumor

jokes.text: Classic wish-maker's mistake: vague language.
jokes.setup: Bart worried his voodoo caused pregnancy instead of stomachache
jokes.punchline: Shauna's matter-of-fact analysis of magical contract law
jokes.explanation: Brilliant casual expertise - treating supernatural mishaps like common mistakes

jokes.text: Fifth grade, you get every dirty detail.
jokes.setup: Saying he can't discuss reproduction with fourth-graders
jokes.punchline: Implying comprehensive sex education happens exactly one grade later
jokes.explanation: Good satire of arbitrary educational boundaries

jokes.text: Oh, everyone does, son. That's why they lock it up in museums where no one will ever visit.
jokes.setup: Bart saying he hates art
jokes.punchline: Homer's cynical view that museums are art prison where no one goes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anti-intellectualism with grain of truth about museum attendance

jokes.text: Not true! Whenever I compliment your virility, you act all weird.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming no one makes a big deal of his procreation
jokes.punchline: Marge revealing she does compliment him but he gets uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Good marital comedy - Marge's earnest appreciation making Homer uncomfortable

jokes.text: Wish I had that Tom Brady's libido.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to change subject to sports
jokes.punchline: Immediately making it sexual again with athlete envy
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity reference humor, not particularly clever

jokes.text: A minute of fun, a lifetime of work.
jokes.setup: Bart being asked to help with chores after brief play
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about childhood work-to-fun ratio
jokes.explanation: Good kid wisdom - captures universal childhood experience with economy

jokes.text: I like to think I'm the boy that makes families.
jokes.setup: Woman asking if he's 'the boy that makes babies'
jokes.punchline: Bart's more wholesome reframing of his alleged power
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart showing unexpected sensitivity about his reputation

jokes.text: Five dollars. Plus one dollar haw-haw insurance.
jokes.setup: Couple asking how much his fertility service costs
jokes.punchline: Adding insurance specifically for mockery
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart entrepreneurship - anticipating his own urge to mock customers

jokes.text: Really glad we're getting a baby with a face. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Bart's spell mentions 'make a baby with a face'
jokes.punchline: Customer's dry appreciation for this basic anatomical feature
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction to absurd spell wording

jokes.text: Haw...! No haw-haw! It worked!
jokes.setup: Bart starts to mock them as predicted
jokes.punchline: Customer stops him mid-haw because pregnancy test is positive
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to insurance joke - Bart almost mocks but gets interrupted by success

jokes.text: Please keep the chatter down while the Womb Wizard is casting his spells.
jokes.setup: Bart has become popular fertility consultant
jokes.punchline: His grandiose self-appointed title 'Womb Wizard'
jokes.explanation: Great self-aggrandizing Bart moment with memorable alliterative title

jokes.text: Barbeque sauce and teddy bear's eye. They want a baby, God knows why.
jokes.setup: Bart performing voodoo spell with random ingredients
jokes.punchline: His cynical aside about people wanting children
jokes.explanation: Good Bart cynicism mixed with absurd spell ingredients

jokes.text: That was my spatula. And you never bought a replacement? Ah, too much aggravation. We just forego flipped food.
jokes.setup: Homer borrowing Flanders' spatula
jokes.punchline: Homer's extreme laziness - giving up all flipped food rather than buy a spatula
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - elaborate lifestyle changes to avoid simple purchase

jokes.text: I'll teach you to answer peoples' prayers!
jokes.setup: Homer angry at Bart for his fertility business
jokes.punchline: Threatening to punish him for helping people
jokes.explanation: Good ironic Homer threat - punishing good deeds because they're inconvenient

jokes.text: Maybe when I've got a dad who shows up in the morning with no shirt on and rocks on his face, it sets, I don't know, a low bar?
jokes.setup: Homer asking what Bart's problem is
jokes.punchline: Bart's specific description of Homer's disheveled morning appearance
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific visual comedy - vivid image of Homer's morning state

jokes.text: Nobody comes into my bar and kidnaps two paying customers. Huh? I must've left my wallet at home. All right, take them, take them!
jokes.setup: Moe heroically defending his customers from kidnappers
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning them when he learns they can't pay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - mercenary priorities overriding customer loyalty

jokes.text: Fill their pockets with corn and toss them to the pigs.
jokes.setup: Mobsters taking Homer and Bart
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and cartoonish execution method
jokes.explanation: Creative threat - mixing rural imagery with mob intimidation

jokes.text: Just do a little of the bunga-bunga, and the hotsie-totsie, and the bop-bada- bop-bada-bop.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony explaining how to make babies
jokes.punchline: His comically euphemistic nonsense words for sex
jokes.explanation: Good Fat Tony character moment - tough guy using silly euphemisms

jokes.text: You sound like every doctor I've ever been to.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony's euphemistic explanation of reproduction
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing it to unhelpful medical advice
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about vague medical explanations

jokes.text: Who has apple breath? You has apple breath.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony talking to his horse Cheesesteak
jokes.punchline: Baby talk to a racehorse about breath
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - tough mobster being tender with his horse

jokes.text: You will breed me a champion by morning or it will be the last sunrise you ever see.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony demanding Bart breed his horses
jokes.punchline: Poetic death threat about sunrises
jokes.explanation: Standard mob threat with slight poetic flourish

jokes.text: Don't over-promise, boss. It might be overcast.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony's poetic threat about last sunrise
jokes.punchline: Henchman's literal concern about weather ruining the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan humor - taking poetic threat literally with practical concerns

jokes.text: It would be helpful if he could go undercover with police horses and find out what they know.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony listing requirements for the horse baby
jokes.punchline: Wanting the foal to be an equine spy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd mob paranoia - treating horses like potential informants

jokes.text: I just did that because it was a series of funny words.
jokes.setup: Bart criticizing Homer's naked Frisbee golf
jokes.punchline: Homer admitting he only did it because the phrase sounded funny
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor - Homer's motivation based purely on comedic sound

jokes.text: I just need to facilitate some horse intercourse.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to be a better father by saving them
jokes.punchline: His plan involves arranging animal breeding
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable comedy from Homer's matter-of-fact approach to crude situation

jokes.text: Big deal. I don't like girls, either. Not like that. It's like this. Smithers... Mr. Largo... Danny Kaye.
jokes.setup: Bart saying the male horse doesn't like girls
jokes.punchline: Bart casually listing local gay men including 1940s entertainer
jokes.explanation: Good Bart observation mixing local and anachronistic references

jokes.text: Ew, that tastes horrible! Beer transformed that cute little boy into the man you see before you.
jokes.setup: Homer spitting out beer and calling it horrible
jokes.punchline: Immediately praising beer's transformative power despite the bad taste
jokes.explanation: Good Homer contradiction - hating beer while crediting it for his manhood

jokes.text: Musical sequence with lyrics about horse romance and their predicament
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart trying to get horses to mate while threatened by mobsters
jokes.punchline: Breaking into elaborate musical number about their bizarre situation
jokes.explanation: Ambitious musical sequence - creative but extended format reduces individual joke impact

jokes.text: The Pimlico plus of joy? Or the Maryland State Fairgrounds minus?
jokes.setup: Fat Tony asking about pregnancy test results
jokes.punchline: Using horse racing venues instead of standard medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Creative character-specific wordplay - Fat Tony's horse obsession coloring everything

jokes.text: The limo's in the shop. We have a loaner. Ay, caramba! You monster! My knees will be in my throat!
jokes.setup: Offer of limo ride home
jokes.punchline: Loaner vehicle is apparently comically small
jokes.explanation: Standard sight gag setup - misdirection about fancy transportation

jokes.text: They'll turn out the same whether you gallop or trot. Teach him not to pee during parades. He'll get more work.
jokes.setup: Marge giving parenting advice to pregnant horse
jokes.punchline: Practical career advice about parade etiquette
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - practical maternal wisdom applied to horses

jokes.text: There's nothing symmetrical about flavor!
jokes.setup: Advertising seven-pack of beer
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical slogan about asymmetrical packaging
jokes.explanation: Absurd advertising logic trying to justify weird packaging

jokes.text: Seven in one night? What kind of a monster could do that? A party monster!
jokes.setup: Ominous question about killing seven in one night
jokes.punchline: Reveal it's about partying and drinking seven beers
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection in beer commercial - sets up murder but delivers partying

tmdb_overview: When Bart's voodoo doll spell accidentally makes his teacher pregnant, he becomes a savior to couples hoping to conceive.
